news.title: STAT+: More workers hate their jobs, but keep them for the health insurance
news.summary: A new survey reveals 24% of U.S. workers stay in unwanted jobs to keep health insurance. Experts call it 'job lock.'

news.title: Trump administration says it’s deferring $1B in Medicaid payments to California and Minnesota
news.summary: The Trump administration said it was deferring more than $1 billion in Medicaid payments to Minnesota and California because of “suspected fraud and noncompliance.”

news.title: STAT+: Rare disease drugmakers try to avoid Trump price cuts
news.summary: Plus, GOP voters want a crackdown on health care fraud, and how PBMs are skirting state laws.

news.title: Opinion: Pete Hegseth, ‘The Odyssey,’ and the testosterone myth
news.summary: The poet didn’t say “sing to me, Muse, of a man with optimized endocrine panels," writes urologist Elizabeth Dray.

news.title: STAT+: Advocates say federal funding is being cut from groups fighting HIV
news.summary: The administration is shifting some funding to states, but community groups are set to lose out.

news.title: Is there an exodus underway at Epic?
news.summary: In this edition of STAT Health Tech: executive departures at Epic Systems, new CEO takes over at Nabla, and more health tech news.

news.title: STAT+: Broad Institute, partners unveil new initiative to develop gene therapies for rare diseases
news.summary: The Broad Institute, Boston Children’s Hospital, and Maine’s Jackson Laboratory announced a collaboration to develop rare disease gene therapies.

news.title: STAT+: Anthropic deepens work with rare disease drugs
news.summary: Novo Nordisk sues Eli Lilly over GLP-1s, Medicare pricing plans, and more biotech news from The Readout

news.title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We’re reading about Novo suing Lilly over ‘deceptive’ ads, a fugitive’s secret life in biotech, and more
news.summary: Novo Nordisk sued Eli Lilly for allegedly running “deceptive” ads to make its obesity and diabetes drugs appear more effective

news.title: STAT+: Nabla’s new CEO on plan to gain market share in a competitive AI scribe environment
news.summary: Ambient scribe developer Nabla said Brian Manning will be the Paris-based company's new CEO as it looks to grow in a highly competitive market.

news.title: STAT+: Novo Nordisk sues Eli Lilly over GLP-1 drug ads it calls ‘deceptive’
news.summary: Novo Nordisk sued Eli Lilly for allegedly running a “deceptive” ad campaign that uses “outdated clinical trials” to make its obesity and diabetes drugs appear more effective.

news.title: A renewed push from the right against abortion pills
news.summary: The latest on cyclospora, THC sodas at the grocery store, and more health news from Morning Rounds

news.title: STAT+: Makers of rare disease drugs seek exemption from Trump’s price-cut pilots
news.summary: A coalition of biotech firms is lobbying to exclude orphan drugs from new federal pilot programs aimed at lowering Medicare drug costs.

news.title: Opinion: Science and the public are learning the wrong lessons from Covid
news.summary: “Post-pandemic polarization threatens to lock our memories into attractive, untrue conclusions that will leave us unprotected next time,” write Marc Lipsitch and Sara Cody.

news.title: Opinion: How a controversial rule could hurt the NIH’s ability to manage risk
news.summary: The proposed rule could “weaken the NIH’s benign influence over research risks,” writes Doni Bloomfield.

news.title: OIG: States can improve enforcement of Medicaid MCOs' fraud referrals
news.summary: As part of the Trump administration's ongoing war on fraud, waste and abuse in healthcare, a new report looks at how states can improve the volume of fraud referrals from managed care organizations.

news.title: Delaware passes laws on hospital price caps, PE purchases, financial assistance
news.summary: A trio of bills signed into law this week outline the latest state-level action on contentious issues affecting healthcare affordability and access.

news.title: Cardinal Health to buy Strive Medical, AdaptHealth's diabetes health business for $360M
news.summary: The pharmaceuticals and specialty products distributor plans to buy AdaptHealth's diabetes health business unit and medical supply provider Strive Medical in separate deals worth $360 million.

news.title: AdventHealth, Intermountain Health plan to form Denver-area joint venture
news.summary: The entity would comprise five AdventHealth hospitals, three Intermountain hospitals and their affiliated sites. It would be managed by AdventHealth, which is also expected to be the joint venture's majority owner.

news.title: T. Denny Sanford, billionaire philanthropist and Sanford Health namesake, dies at 90
news.summary: The Midwest native gave more than $4 billion over the course of his lifetime, including nearly $2 billion to Sanford Health.

news.title: Trump admin pausing over $1B in Medicaid payments to California, Minnesota
news.summary: The funding freezes are due to “suspected fraud and noncompliance,” U.S. Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. said in a Tuesday press conference.

news.title: Karoo Health banks $16.2M series A to advance value-based cardiology platform
news.summary: The company currently supports a network across 11 states made up of more than 600 cardiology providers, executives say.

news.title: Health systems need to prepare for an AI-driven reinvention of work, Abundant Alliance's Eric Langshur says
news.summary: The Abundant Alliance is pursuing a number of initiatives to upskill the workforce on AI use, build governance frameworks and streamline physician credentialing and privileging.

news.title: DHS decouples duration of some visas from training program length
news.summary: About one in 10 physicians in a residency or fellowship program are sponsored under a J visa. Many training programs run longer than the new rule's four-year cap, thereby requiring students to apply for an Extension of Stay with the government.

news.title: Healthcare bankruptcy volume 'stabilizes' in H1, though more practices are falling under
news.summary: Quarterly bankruptcy filings appear to be leveling out after years of volatility, which "is surprising considering the on-the-ground pressures we see operators facing every day," a Gibbins Advisors principal said.

news.title: AI emerges as top healthcare IT investment globally, KLAS finds
news.summary: AI was named the top global healthcare IT investment priority among 57% of respondents, followed by EHRs and digitalization.

news.title: Healthcare affordability, fraud are key issues for voters heading into 2026 midterms
news.summary: Healthcare affordability is still the leading overall issue for voters heading into midterms, though another Trump administration talking point is gaining steam, according to a KFF survey report.

news.title: Why Novartis CEO ended his ADC holdout for $1.5B Myricx deal
news.summary: For years, as Big Pharma poured billions into ADCs, Novartis CEO Vas Narasimhan notably stayed on the sidelines. Asked by Fierce during Novartis’ Q2 earnings call, Narasimhan explained why he is ending that holdout with the proposed $1.5 billion deal for Myricx.

news.title: Jupiter enters psychedelics space with $100M deal for PharmAla's MDMA drug
news.summary: Jupiter Neurosciences will pay $3.3 million upfront to Canada's PharmAla Biotech for U.S. rights to an MDMA drug to treat neuropsychiatric disorders.

news.title: Agios says adios to sickle cell program over lackluster phase 2 data
news.summary: A phase 2 study of Agios Pharmaceuticals’ next-generation pyruvate kinase activator has failed to support continued development in sickle cell disease, leading the company to ax an indication for the second time in two months.

news.title: Indian generics maker Lupin seeds US spinout Kaveri with cash, clinical-phase cancer assets
news.summary: Lupin has spun out two clinical-phase cancer drug candidates to create Kaveri Therapeutics, a U.S.-based startup that plans to raise cash for further development. Kaveri’s programs hit targets pursued by major drugmakers including AstraZeneca, Bayer and Bristol Myers Squibb.

news.title: Novartis drops phase 2 radioligand therapy, but CEO’s commitment to modality ‘unchanged’
news.summary: Novartis has dropped a phase 2-stage radioligand therapy after the candidate disappointed in the clinic.

news.title: Genetic medicine luminaries launch new center for personalized therapies
news.summary: A team of veteran scientists from elite institutions has come together to form a new center dedicated to the crafting of personalized genetic medicines, adding to the growing list of similar efforts in the wake of Baby KJ’s groundbreaking custom CRISPR treatment.

news.title: ‘There is always a first’: Why US biotech Axiom will begin its IPO journey in Hong Kong
news.summary: Axiom Biosciences intends to pursue an IPO on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange (HKEX), followed by a co-listing on the Nasdaq—a strategy the company claims is the first U.S. biotech to use.

news.title: Roche taps Foundation Medicine’s chief as new North America Diagnostics lead, creates new commercial role
news.summary: Dan Malarek is leaving his role as chief executive of Roche’s sequencing subsidiary Foundation Medicine as he takes up the mantle as President and CEO of Roche Diagnostics North America.

news.title: BMS expands AI footprint with Nvidia, aiming for ‘most powerful AI factory in life sciences’
news.summary: Bristol Myers Squibb has entered into an expanded collaboration with Nvidia to create the biggest supercomputer for drug R&D, according to the announcement.

news.title: Tempus buys out cancer genomics partner Personalis in $1.5 billion deal
news.summary: Tempus is boosting its cancer offerings with a deal to buy out Personalis three years after the pair first teamed up in oncology.

news.title: Scribe aims for $96M IPO to fund clinical push for lipid-lowering genetic meds
news.summary: Scribe Therapeutics has put its IPO plans down on paper, showing the genetic medicine company hopes to raise $96 million through its public listing.

news.title: Kolon’s cell-based gene therapy fails phase 3 osteoarthritis trial
news.summary: A phase 3 trial of Kolon TissueGene’s cell-based gene therapy has missed its co-primary endpoints. With a second late-stage study scheduled to report data in October, the biotech is holding off on determining the next steps for the osteoarthritis candidate.

news.title: 昌平2026脑科学与脑机接口创新论坛举办 芯智达、智冉医疗等10余家企业入驻脑机接口集聚区
news.summary: 2026脑科学与脑机接口创新论坛圆满举办 25+顶尖院士专家、领军企业共探产业落地通路

news.title: “AI吃AI会完蛋”是伪命题，真正的产品风险是缺乏数据纠错机制
news.summary: 当AI开始吞噬自己的输出，模型坍塌的阴影笼罩行业。

news.title: 基于超1万肿瘤样本训练，哈佛医学院等提出泛癌症基础模型COMPASS，平均性能优于22种现有方法
news.summary: 在不同队列中平均将预测准确率提升了 8.5%

news.title: 当AI给出标准答案，人如何“不同凡想”？一场WAIC UP! 思享会撕开的全球追问
news.summary: AI时代人生无标准答案，各方共探价值与方向

papers.title: ReViV: Reconstructing the Viewer and the View in 4D from Monocular Egocentric Video
papers.summary: Egocentric devices, such as wearable front-facing cameras, provide a unique perspective for capturing the continuous interaction between a human viewer and the surrounding environment. A holistic and efficient multimodal model capable of reconstructing this 4D representation is therefore highly desirable. However, existing approaches often rely on auxiliary inputs such as pre-computed camera trajectories, treat scene perception and human ego-motion modeling as separate problems despite their strong interdependency, and suffer from slow inference time. To address these limitations, we present R

papers.title: Do Language Models Dream of Binding Molecules? Benchmarking LLMs under Spatial Constraints
papers.summary: Structure-based drug design (SBDD) leverages the 3D structure of protein targets, often complemented by other spatial constraints, to generate candidate binding molecules. While diffusion models have dominated as a leading paradigm for high-quality 3D molecule generation, LLM-based methods are rapidly emerging in molecular design and have shown competitive performance in pocket-conditioned molecular generation. However, their ability to reason about physics and 3D spatial environments is largely underexplored. In this work, we systematically analyze whether current general-purpose LLMs are cap

papers.title: Diagnosing and Calibrating Tool-Call Boundary Drift in Multi-Teacher On-Policy Distillation
papers.summary:
Agentic language models must learn when to call tools, when to consume tool responses, and when to answer directly. This makes multi-teacher on-policy distillation a natural training strategy: one teacher can specialize in tool calls, another in direct responses, and the student can learn from both on
  its own generated distribution. We show that this strategy can induce a behavior shift that is invisible from aggregate losses alone. In a two-teacher tool-use setting, vanilla generalized knowledge distillation improves tool-call recall but also moves the model toward over-calling, where it ca

papers.title: Can Multimodal Large Language Models Understand OCT?
papers.summary: Optical coherence tomography (OCT) imaging is essential for the diagnosis and treatment of retinal diseases. Although multimodal large language models (MLLMs) have demonstrated considerable potential in medical image analysis, existing benchmarks largely reduce OCT understanding to coarse-grained disease classification or isolated visual question answering, leaving the complete cognitive process from visual perception to clinical reasoning insufficiently evaluated. To address this limitation, we introduce OCT-Bench, a comprehensive benchmark dedicated to OCT image understanding. OCT-Bench comp

papers.title: Benchmarking Sensor Robustness in Plasma Diagnostic Models: A Systematic Evaluation on TokaMark
papers.summary: Plasma diagnostic models for tokamak fusion devices are almost universally evaluated on clean, complete sensor data. In practice, fusion diagnostics fail regularly: acquisition systems start late, individual sensors die, and signal dropouts cluster precisely when a plasma disruption is approaching. We present the first systematic robustness benchmark for plasma diagnostic ML using the TokaMark dataset of 11,573 MAST shots, evaluating XGBoost, LSTM, Transformer, and the TokaMark CNN baseline across six physically-grounded failure scenarios and three imputation strategies. We introduce the Robus
